violations.text: The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Scoop was adjusted for proper storage at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food-contact surfaces of equipment are to be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and preclude the build-up of debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety that is licensed/registered by the RIDOH Center for Food Protection. PIC is to apply as soon as possible. Application printed for PIC at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 02/27/2024

The following sources of possible contamination were observed... - The tubing to the cold plate in the ice well was observe to be in contact with the ice used for drink service. The ice used for drink service is to be separated/protected from the ice in contact with the tubing to the cold plate. - The drain tube to the soda gun was observed to be draining into the drain of the downstairs bar ice well. The soda gun drain tubing is to be kept separate and drain into the plumbing below. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2024
The facility is lacking thermo-labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine at the upstairs bar area. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing tablets used at the kitchen 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2024

The ice wells at both the upstairs and downstairs bars are lacking a proper air gap (currently equipped with an air break). A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2024
